evidence: All five handler classes (ConcordanceHandlers, CorpusQueryHandlers, ExplorationHandlers, SketchHandlers, VisualizationHandlers) follow an identical structural pattern: package-private class, no public interface, single-field-per-dependency constructor, SLF4J logger, void handle* methods — perfectly consistent across the api package

All static-method utility classes (RelationPatternUtils, RelationUtils, CqlUtils, LogDiceUtils, MathUtils, GrammarConfigSerializer, ExploreResponseAssembler) follow the same idiom: public final class, private no-arg constructor, pure static methods — no drift between config, utils, and api packages

All four enum types with JSON labels (RelationEdgeType, SharingCategory, PosGroup, RelationType) consistently use a label field, a label() accessor, and a toString() override — same pattern repeated without exception
Every package in the tree has a package-info.java file: api, config, exploration, indexer/blacklab, model, model/exploration, query, utils — no package is undocumented

RadialPlot.java sits in the api package alongside HTTP handlers; the api package-info says 'HTTP request handlers, routing utilities, and response assembly' — RadialPlot is a pure SVG rendering class with no HTTP concerns, formerly in a viz/ package (per CLAUDE.md). No rationale documents why it was moved into api rather than a dedicated rendering package

ComparisonResult uses a public constructor (new ComparisonResult(nouns, collocates)) while sibling ExplorationResult uses a private-field approach with no private constructor annotation and a static factory empty(). The model/package-info explains why both are classes rather than records, but does not address the construction-pattern asymmetry between them

ExplorationOptions Javadoc line 8 reads 'Single-seed exploration uses SingleSeedExplorationOptions, which adds the reverseExpansionLimit parameter specific to the reverse-lookup phase' — no such class exists in the codebase; exploreByPattern takes reverseExpansionLimit as a plain int parameter. This is stale documentation left over from a planned but unimplemented extraction

strengths: Handler class structure is uniform across all five api handlers: same visibility, same constructor shape, same logging idiom, no deviations
Static utility classes follow an identical final-class-with-private-constructor pattern in every package that has them
Enum-with-label pattern (label field + label() accessor + toString() override) applied uniformly to all four domain enums
Every package has a package-info.java, providing consistent discoverability and documentation coverage
Model type strategy (record vs class) is consciously documented in model/package-info.java, even if the rationale does not extend to construction-pattern consistency
convention_outlier.issue_character: The issues are isolated outliers from otherwise strong, codebase-wide patterns rather than systemic drift: a misplaced rendering class, an undocumented asymmetry in construction style between two sibling model classes, and a single stale Javadoc cross-reference.
convention_outlier.score_rationale: The codebase demonstrates exemplary convention discipline in its most-used patterns — handler shape, utility class shape, enum idiom, and package documentation are all applied without a single deviation. What prevents a higher score is a small cluster of localized outliers: RadialPlot belongs to the api package despite being a rendering utility with no HTTP concerns (a style island relative to the rest of api), ComparisonResult and ExplorationResult diverge on construction convention without explanation, and one Javadoc references a class that was never implemented. These issues would momentarily mislead a developer exploring the api package or comparing the two result types, placing the codebase solidly in the 'solid but uneven' band rather than exemplary.

issues.summary: RadialPlot (SVG renderer) colocated with HTTP handlers in the api package — style island with no documented rationale
evidence: api/package-info.java describes the package as 'HTTP request handlers, routing utilities, and response assembly'
RadialPlot contains no HTTP code — it is a pure SVG layout algorithm (spiral math, guide circles, label positioning) with no dependency on HttpExchange or any http class
CLAUDE.md records the class was formerly at viz/RadialPlot.java, indicating a prior package existed and was dissolved without a documented rationale for the new location

All other api package classes are either handlers (ConcordanceHandlers et al.), routing infrastructure (WordSketchApiServer, HttpApiUtils), or response assembly utilities (ExploreResponseAssembler) — RadialPlot fits none of these roles
issues.suggestion: Move RadialPlot to a dedicated rendering or visualization package (e.g. pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.viz or pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.api.render) and update api/package-info.java to note that visualization rendering lives in the viz sub-package. Update the import in VisualizationHandlers accordingly.

issues.summary: ComparisonResult uses a public constructor while sibling ExplorationResult uses factory methods — undocumented construction-pattern asymmetry within the same model sub-package
evidence: ExplorationResult has a public constructor but also a static factory empty(String seed) and is documented as favouring the factory for the empty-sentinel case; the class Javadoc emphasises 'factory keeps the construction logic encapsulated'
ComparisonResult also has a public constructor AND a static factory empty(), matching the same dual-pattern, but neither class makes explicit that the factory is preferred over the constructor

model/package-info.java explains why both are classes rather than records, but does not address which construction entry-point callers should prefer, leaving both constructors public and both factories present with no clear contract
issues.suggestion: Align construction convention: either make both constructors package-private (factory-only access) or document in model/package-info.java that public constructors are intentional for both types and the factory methods cover only the empty-sentinel case. The former is more consistent with the stated encapsulation intent in ExplorationResult's Javadoc.

issues.summary: ExplorationOptions Javadoc references non-existent class SingleSeedExplorationOptions — stale cross-reference from an unimplemented extraction
evidence: ExplorationOptions.java line 8: 'Single-seed exploration uses {@link SingleSeedExplorationOptions}, which adds the reverseExpansionLimit parameter'
No class named SingleSeedExplorationOptions exists anywhere in the repository
ExplorationService.exploreByPattern takes reverseExpansionLimit as a plain int parameter, not via a specialised options class — the extraction described in the Javadoc was never carried out
issues.suggestion: Remove the {@link SingleSeedExplorationOptions} reference from ExplorationOptions Javadoc and replace with an accurate description: e.g. 'Single-seed exploration passes the additional reverseExpansionLimit parameter directly to ExplorationService.exploreByPattern rather than via a subtype of this record.'

root_causes: RadialPlot migration from viz/ to api/ was done for convenience (it is only used by VisualizationHandlers) without establishing a principled placement policy, and no documentation records the decision

The model type strategy documentation in package-info.java was written to justify record-vs-class choice but was not extended to cover construction conventions, leaving two classes in the same package with subtly different idioms
ExplorationOptions Javadoc was written prospectively during a refactor that planned to extract a SingleSeedExplorationOptions subtype; when the extraction was abandoned the Javadoc was not updated
